content:
# Qalibah Formation

**Summary**: A Silurian formation in the Northwest Saudi Arabia.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-mark|Mark, 2013]] studied the Qalibah formation for overpressure detection.

- [[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017]] studied the qalibah-formation to characterize its mineralogy and response to drilling fluids.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2013-mark|Mark, 2013]] marine

- [[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017]] studied the qalibah-formation to characterize its mineralogy and response to drilling fluids.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2013-mark|Mark, 2013]] High pore pressure in the lower sections.

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Qusaiba member is a source rock of over 90% of Paleozoic hydrocarbons and acts as a seal to underlying reservoirs. ([[2013-mark|Mark, 2013, p. 44]])

- Samples A, B, C, and D showed varying levels of clay content and silica richness. ([[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017, p. 68]])
